text: AIRA is a web-based Artificial Intelligence Requirement Analyzer that helps users create Software Requirements Specification documents, generate UML diagrams, analyze SRS ambiguity, describe UML diagram images, and download professional reports from one workspace.

name: Content Placeholder 2
text:
Generate complete SRS documents from project details or uploaded files.
Create professional UML diagrams and allow editing.

name: Title 1
text: Problem Statement
textPreview: Problem Statement

name: Content Placeholder 2
text: Manual requirement documentation is time-consuming and error-prone. Ambiguous requirements, missing actors, weak UML diagrams, and inconsistent formatting can reduce software quality. Users need a single system that can generate, analyze, edit, and export SRS and UML outputs professionally.
textPreview: Manual requirement documentation is time-consuming and error-prone. Ambiguous requirements, missing actors, weak UML diagrams, and incons...

name: Title 1
text: Proposed Solution
textPreview: Proposed Solution

name: Content Placeholder 2
text: AIRA provides an integrated web application where users can enter a project idea or upload files, generate a structured SRS, create editable UML diagrams, analyze requirement ambiguity, describe UML images, save history, manage account settings, and download PDF, Word, and TXT outputs.

name: Title 1
text: Functional Requirements
textPreview: Functional Requirements

name: Content Placeholder 2
text:
The system shall allow users to register and log in securely.
The system shall generate SRS documents from text, files, or diagrams.
The system shall generate and edit UML diagrams.
The system shall analyze SRS ambiguity and show recommendations.
The system shall describe UML images and export reports.
The system shall store user history and enforce free/premium limits.

name: AIRA Database Summary
text:
Database: MySQL.
Key tables include users, uploaded files, UML requests, UML outputs, saved history, usage records, and plan/subscription information. Data is used for login, account display, history retrieval, usage limits, and downloadable outputs.

textPreview: Department of Computer Science & Information Technology, University of Chakwal

name: AIRA Architecture Summary
text:
Frontend: HTML, CSS, JavaScript pages.
Backend: Node.js and Express APIs.
Database: MySQL.
AI layer: Gemini/OpenRouter-based generation and analysis fallback.
Exports: PDF, Word, and TXT report generation.

name: AIRA Sequence Summary
text: Typical flow: User enters a prompt or uploads a file, frontend sends request to backend, backend validates input, AI service generates SRS/UML/analysis output, output is saved in database/history, and user downloads or edits the result.
